---
title: "1.XPCLR analysis (domestication and modern breeding)"
output: html_notebook
author: "subhash"
date: "2026-03-05"
---

# make sure to load modules on HCC unl

This file records what and how we ran XPCLR in sorghum.

comparisons used:
1. domestication stage: landrace vs wild
2. modern breeding stage: improved vs landrace

we used these XPCLR parameters:
- window size = 25000 bp
- step size = 5000 bp
- max SNPs per window = 200
- LD cutoff = 0.95
- recombination fallback = 1e-8 per bp

## 1) creating organized directories and clean sample lists

```{bash}
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

ml bcftools

BASE=/work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/xpclr
VCF=/work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/filterfed_vcf/SorGSD.289snp.miss05.vcf.gz
RAW=/work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/filterfed_vcf

mkdir -p ${BASE}
mkdir -p ${BASE}/sample_lists
mkdir -p ${BASE}/logs
mkdir -p ${BASE}/domestication_landrace_vs_wild
mkdir -p ${BASE}/breeding_improved_vs_landrace

# get all sample IDs from vcf
bcftools query -l "${VCF}" > ${BASE}/sample_lists/vcf_samples.txt

# optional check for sample IDs not present in VCF
comm -23 <(sort ${RAW}/landrace.txt) <(sort ${BASE}/sample_lists/vcf_samples.txt) || true
comm -23 <(sort ${RAW}/wild.txt) <(sort ${BASE}/sample_lists/vcf_samples.txt) || true
comm -23 <(sort ${RAW}/improved.txt) <(sort ${BASE}/sample_lists/vcf_samples.txt) || true

# clean lists (only IDs present in VCF)
grep -Fxf ${BASE}/sample_lists/vcf_samples.txt ${RAW}/landrace.txt > ${BASE}/sample_lists/landrace.clean.txt
grep -Fxf ${BASE}/sample_lists/vcf_samples.txt ${RAW}/wild.txt > ${BASE}/sample_lists/wild.clean.txt
grep -Fxf ${BASE}/sample_lists/vcf_samples.txt ${RAW}/improved.txt > ${BASE}/sample_lists/improved.clean.txt

wc -l ${BASE}/sample_lists/landrace.clean.txt ${BASE}/sample_lists/wild.clean.txt ${BASE}/sample_lists/improved.clean.txt
```

## 2) tiny smoke test before full submit

```{bash}
#!/bin/bash
ml bcftools

mkdir -p /work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/xpclr_test

bcftools view \
  -t 1:1-200000 \
  -Oz \
  -o /work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/xpclr_test/test.chr1.1_200kb.vcf.gz \
  /work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/filterfed_vcf/SorGSD.289snp.miss05.vcf.gz

bcftools index -t /work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/xpclr_test/test.chr1.1_200kb.vcf.gz
```

run test command:

```{bash}
#!/bin/bash
ml xpclr

VCF=/work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/xpclr_test/test.chr1.1_200kb.vcf.gz
OUT=/work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/xpclr_test/landrace_vs_wild.test.chr1.tsv

xpclr \
  --format vcf \
  --input "${VCF}" \
  --samplesA /work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/xpclr/sample_lists/landrace.clean.txt \
  --samplesB /work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/xpclr/sample_lists/wild.clean.txt \
  --chr 1 \
  --size 25000 \
  --step 5000 \
  --maxsnps 200 \
  --ld 0.95 \
  --rrate 1e-8 \
  --out "${OUT}"
```

 output is generated and no assertion error, then submited full jobs.

## 3) slurm script for domestication run (landrace vs wild)

save as `xpclr_domestication.slurm`

```{bash}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=xpclr_dom
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=1
#SBATCH --mem=16G
#SBATCH --time=72:00:00
#SBATCH --partition=batch,guest,jclarke
#SBATCH --output=/work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/xpclr/logs/%x_%A_%a.out
#SBATCH --error=/work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/xpclr/logs/%x_%A_%a.err
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --mail-user=smahamkalivenkatas2@huskers.unl.edu
#SBATCH --array=1-10

set -euo pipefail

ml bcftools
ml xpclr

# to make sure tabix is available
which tabix || true

CHR="${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID}"
BASE=/work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/xpclr
VCF=/work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/filterfed_vcf/SorGSD.289snp.miss05.vcf.gz
OUTDIR=${BASE}/domestication_landrace_vs_wild

mkdir -p "${OUTDIR}"

xpclr \
  --format vcf \
  --input "${VCF}" \
  --samplesA ${BASE}/sample_lists/landrace.clean.txt \
  --samplesB ${BASE}/sample_lists/wild.clean.txt \
  --chr "${CHR}" \
  --size 25000 \
  --step 5000 \
  --maxsnps 200 \
  --ld 0.95 \
  --rrate 1e-8 \
  --out "${OUTDIR}/domestication_landrace_vs_wild.chr${CHR}.tsv"
```

submit:

```{bash}
sbatch xpclr_domestication.slurm
```

## 4) slurm script for breeding run (improved vs landrace)

save as `xpclr_breeding.slurm`

```{bash}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=xpclr_breed
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=1
#SBATCH --mem=16G
#SBATCH --time=72:00:00
#SBATCH --partition=batch,guest,jclarke
#SBATCH --output=/work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/xpclr/logs/%x_%A_%a.out
#SBATCH --error=/work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/xpclr/logs/%x_%A_%a.err
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --mail-user=smahamkalivenkatas2@huskers.unl.edu
#SBATCH --array=1-10

set -euo pipefail

ml bcftools
ml xpclr

which tabix || true

CHR="${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID}"
BASE=/work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/xpclr
VCF=/work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/filterfed_vcf/SorGSD.289snp.miss05.vcf.gz
OUTDIR=${BASE}/breeding_improved_vs_landrace

mkdir -p "${OUTDIR}"

xpclr \
  --format vcf \
  --input "${VCF}" \
  --samplesA ${BASE}/sample_lists/improved.clean.txt \
  --samplesB ${BASE}/sample_lists/landrace.clean.txt \
  --chr "${CHR}" \
  --size 25000 \
  --step 5000 \
  --maxsnps 200 \
  --ld 0.95 \
  --rrate 1e-8 \
  --out "${OUTDIR}/breeding_improved_vs_landrace.chr${CHR}.tsv"
```

submit:

```{bash}
sbatch xpclr_breeding.slurm
```

## 5) job monitoring and completion checks

```{bash}
# running jobs
squeue -u subhash

# full status by array task
sacct -j <jobid> --format=JobID,State,ExitCode

```

final output files:

```{bash}
ls /work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/xpclr/domestication_landrace_vs_wild/*.tsv | wc -l
ls /work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/xpclr/breeding_improved_vs_landrace/*.tsv | wc -l
```

## 6) merge all chromosomes after completion

```{bash}
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

BASE=/work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/xpclr

for label in domestication_landrace_vs_wild breeding_improved_vs_landrace; do
  out="${BASE}/${label}/${label}.all_chr.tsv"
  first=1
  for chr in {1..10}; do
    f="${BASE}/${label}/${label}.chr${chr}.tsv"
    if [ ${first} -eq 1 ]; then
      cat "${f}" > "${out}"
      first=0
    else
      tail -n +2 "${f}" >> "${out}"
    fi
  done
  echo "merged: ${out}"
done
```

## 7) quick manhattan plotting columns

use these columns from XPCLR output:
1. `chrom` for chromosome
2. position = midpoint of `start` and `stop`
3. `xpclr` as y-axis

example R snippet:

```{r}
library(data.table)

x <- fread("/work/jyanglab/subhash/sorgsd/xpclr/domestication_landrace_vs_wild/domestication_landrace_vs_wild.all_chr.tsv")
x[, pos := (start + stop) / 2]

# keep only needed columns
man <- x[, .(chrom, pos, xpclr)]
head(man)
```

# note
This workflow is pairwise selection scan. It is different from BalLeRMix workflow (DAF based balancing selection).
